<!-- Generated by a 27-agent audit workflow (10 subsystem audits + adversarial verification + cross-cut structure critic + objective metrics + 3-architect PydanticAI panel). Date: 2026-06-15. Branch: fix/general-detector-prompt-deleak. -->

# CloakBot Privacy Kernel — Code Audit

## Executive Summary

The CloakBot privacy kernel is **production-grade in its security posture but not yet in its structure**: the raw-value trust boundary holds across all ten subsystems — only placeholders and structured JSON cross to the remote/local-model paths, raw values stay in the session Vault, telemetry logs counts/types not values, and the default everywhere is fail-closed. After adversarial verification, **zero findings represent an actual leak, correctness defect, or boundary violation**; every confirmed item is a maintainability, type-honesty, or idiom concern. The biggest strengths are the streaming sanitizer/restorer (exemplary invariant-stating single-responsibility modules), the WebUI single-chokepoint egress gate, and the consistently threat-modeled docstrings. The biggest risks are *latent*, not live: a placeholder-token grammar hand-copied across **7+ modules** (drift would silently weaken HIGH-severity detection), a 1156-line `visual_redaction.py` god-module that concentrates half the complexity hotspots, and pervasive `Any` typing that hides the privacy-critical sanitized-vs-restored distinction. Because the owner intends to rewrite onto PydanticAI, the highest-leverage cleanups (centralizing the regex, deleting dead code, then folding the hand-rolled LLM-JSON runners into typed Agents) double as migration prep. This is a careful, security-conscious kernel that needs a consolidation pass, not a rescue.

## Scorecard

Scores are 1–5 (higher is better) per the per-subsystem reviews.

| Subsystem | Structure | Readability | Conciseness | PEP8/Idiom | One-line takeaway |
|---|:--:|:--:|:--:|:--:|---|
| detection-core | 4 | 4 | 3 | 4 | Clean facade + layering; drag is dead `llm_json` helpers and duplicated token regex/parsers. |
| chunking | 5 | 4 | 3 | 4 | Best-layered subsystem; one latent `char_span` metadata trap in an unconsumed field. |
| sanitization-state | 3 | 4 | 3 | 4 | Security-conscious and correct, but `vault.py` is an 839-line four-responsibility god-module. |
| math | 4 | 4 | 3 | 4 | Sound AST-allowlist arithmetic kernel; duplicated regex/cleanup/substitution helpers + dead exports. |
| agents-routing | 3 | 4 | 3 | 4 | Correct dispatch fragmented across two single-function modules; supported-intent set stated 3×. |
| runtime | 4 | 4 | 3 | 4 | Exemplary streaming modules; `pipeline.py` emit-boilerplate and duplicated tool-interceptor builders drag it. |
| hooks-protocol-prompting | 3 | 4 | 3 | 4 | Hooks/prompting/transparency production-grade; `protocol/` ships a dead aspirational contract schema as public API. |
| visual-redaction | 3 | 4 | 3 | 4 | Fail-closed and correct; `visual_redaction.py` is a 1156-line six-concern god-module with no `__all__`. |
| egress-compaction-misc | 4 | 4 | 3 | 3 | Strong additive-wrapper pattern; weakened by private-attr reach-through into `FallbackProvider` and pervasive `Any`. |
| webui | 4 | 5 | 4 | 4 | Cleanest subsystem; single-chokepoint egress gate, minor `__all__`/dual-serializer hygiene gaps. |

## Top Findings (Prioritized)

No finding survived verification at critical/high severity — the adversarial pass downgraded every initially-high item once it confirmed the trust boundary was intact. The list below is the set of **`medium`** findings (the genuine cleanup priorities), ordered by blast radius. Nitpick `low` items are deferred to later sections and the action plan.

---

**`MEDIUM` · Placeholder-token grammar duplicated across 7+ modules with drift** — `core/state/vault.py:38-39`, `core/sanitization/handler.py:11`, `provider_egress_gate.py:34`, `core/detection/detector.py:25`, `core/detection/digit_detector.py:13`, `core/detection/general_detector.py:12`, `core/math/math_executor.py:18`, plus the inline literal at `core/sanitization/restorer.py:165` *(SAN-002, ECM-05, DUP-001)*
The `<<TAG_N>>` grammar is the security-relevant gate for the whole placeholder trust boundary, yet it is re-typed in at least seven places with deliberate-but-divergent variants (`[A-Z]{2,}` in detectors vs `[A-Z]+` elsewhere; anchored `fullmatch` vs unanchored `search`; with/without capture group). The objective grep counts **81** placeholder-pattern occurrences tree-wide. **Why it matters:** no current bug, but a future tag-format change must be hand-synced across every copy; missing one would silently stop matching a placeholder in *one* path (e.g. stop stripping a HIGH-severity token before egress) without any test failing. **Fix:** define the grammar once near where tokens are minted (`core/placeholders.py` or `core/types.py`), exposing a `PLACEHOLDER_RE` (search), an anchored capturing `TOKEN_RE` (tag/index extraction), and a `PLACEHOLDER_TAG_RE` (capture). Import everywhere; delete `_IS_PLACEHOLDER_RE` (redundant with `PLACEHOLDER_RE.fullmatch`) and the egress-gate/restorer copies.

---

**`MEDIUM` · Span-overlap protection logic duplicated between `handler.py` and `vault.replace_known_originals`** — `core/sanitization/handler.py:14-39,124-127` and `core/state/vault.py:315-345` *(SAN-004)*
The privacy-critical "don't overwrite inside an existing placeholder" overlap predicate (`s < end and start < e`) is written twice: `handler.py` builds protected spans + right-to-left replacement via `_find_protected_spans`/`_overlaps_any`/`_find_safe_positions`, while `vault.replace_known_originals` re-implements the identical interval logic inline. **Why it matters:** this is the placeholder-protection invariant; two copies mean one can regress independently and re-expose a raw original inside a token. **Fix:** extract a shared `replace_non_overlapping(text, needle, replacement) -> tuple[str, bool]` (or a small span-helper module) consumed by both `apply_tokens` and `replace_known_originals`, with one test pinning the overlap rule.

---

**`MEDIUM` · Two hand-maintained `ToolPrivacyRecord` builders must stay in sync** — `runtime/tool_interceptor.py:342-353` and `:440-451` *(RT-002)*
`sanitize_tool_result` and `_record_streaming_result` build the record with the same field list (`tool_call_id`, `tool_name`, `privacy_class`, `remote_arguments=dict(...)`, `sanitized_output=_recorded_output_text(...)`, `was_sanitized=...`) and both `extend` `tool_output_entities`; only `visual_redactions`/`vault_artifacts` differ. **Why it matters:** a new transparency-record field added to one path and forgotten in the other yields inconsistent privacy records. **Fix:** extract `_append_tool_record(tool_call, sanitized, modified, entities, privacy_class, *, visual_redactions=(), vault_artifacts=())`.

---

**`MEDIUM` · Two near-identical approval-gate blocks in `prepare_tool_call`** — `runtime/tool_interceptor.py:246-260` and `:274-288` *(RT-003)*
The non-LOCAL-sensitive branch and the high-severity-LOCAL branch each build a 9-field `ToolApprovalRequest`, append to `tool_approvals`, and raise `ToolApprovalRequiredError`; only `remote_arguments` (sanitized vs restored) differs. The privacy distinction is correct, but it is buried in copy-paste. **Why it matters:** the sanitized-vs-restored choice *is* the boundary decision and should be the single visible difference, not hidden in two large duplicated blocks. **Fix:** factor `_raise_for_approval(tool_call, *, remote_arguments, restored_arguments, entities, privacy_class)`; each branch keeps only its trigger and its `remote_arguments` choice.

---

**`MEDIUM` · Hand-rolled recursive 5-tuple sanitizer is fragile to positional drift** — `runtime/tool_interceptor.py:469-549` *(RT-005)*
`_sanitize_value` returns `(sanitized, modified, entities, visual_redactions, failed)`; the list and dict branches each re-implement the same fold, and callers track five positional slots with throwaways (`_visual_redactions`, `_failed`, `_vr`). **Why it matters:** a positional mismatch in the fail-closed propagation chain is easy to introduce and hard to spot. **Fix:** return a frozen `SanitizeResult` dataclass with a `merge()` classmethod folding `any()` over bools and extending lists; both branches collapse to one merge.

---

**`MEDIUM` · `visual_redaction.py` is a 1156-line god-module with no `__all__`** — `visual_redaction.py:1-1156` *(VR-001, VR-002)*
One module owns label/tag tables, the remote detector prompt + `chat.completions` call, hand-rolled JSON parsing, OCR fuzzy/regex matching, the vault-orchestrating `process_visual_blocks`, region-map text rendering, and PIL box-drawing — and, uniquely among privacy modules, defines no `__all__`, so ~40 underscore helpers leak into the public surface of the most-imported-from module in the subsystem. It concentrates 3 of 6 C901 hotspots. **Why it matters:** agent legibility (a repo core belief) and review surface; this is the file most likely to hide a future regression. **Fix:** split into a `visual_redaction/` package (`detector.py`, `ocr_match.py`, `render.py`, `pipeline.py`) and add an explicit `__all__` to the public boundary.

---

**`MEDIUM` · WebUI redactors hand-reconstruct fields with no coverage invariant** — `webui/side_channel.py:58-185` *(webui-sec-001)*
Each `_redact_*` re-lists source-model fields by hand. Verification confirmed this **fails closed** (a new un-handled field defaults to `None`/empty, it is not leaked), so this is defense-in-depth, not a live leak. **Why it matters:** a developer could forget to null a field, and there is no test ensuring handled fields == `model_fields`. **Fix:** add a test asserting per-model field coverage, or mark raw fields via `Field` metadata so the redactor projection is derivable rather than hand-listed.

---

## Structure & Organization

**Layering and dependency direction are sound.** Across every subsystem the low-level modules do not import the high-level ones: `llm_json`/`types` have no upward imports, `tool_detector` correctly sits above `PiiDetector`, the streaming modules never import `pipeline`, and the chunkers are the cleanest layer in the tree (structure 5/5). No dependency cycles cause runtime problems; the lazy/deferred imports that exist (`visual_egress_gate.py:287-289`, `process_visual_blocks` body) are deliberate cycle-avoidance, not accidents — though their commenting is inconsistent (VR-010).

**The recurring structural anti-pattern is the god-module / god-object.** Three files concentrate the structural debt:
- `visual_redaction.py` (1156 LOC, six concerns — VR-001).
- `core/state/vault.py` (839 LOC) fuses four unrelated responsibilities that change for different reasons and have different test surfaces: the entity/computation registry data model, the Cap-B ephemeral-scope routing machinery with thread-local state, disk persistence/atomic-write/prune, and artifact byte/text storage (SAN-001). Proposed split: keep the registry in `vault.py`; move scope routing to `scope.py`; move disk + artifact I/O to `vault_store.py`, with `get_map`/`save_map` as the re-export facade.
- `runtime/pipeline.py` `PrivacyRuntime` trends toward a god-object mixing turn orchestration with media/document decoding (RT-004). Note the verification caveat: the worst-cited examples (`_decode_data_url`, `_document_suffix`) are *already* module-level free functions, so the real residue is only `_build_image_blocks_from_media`/`_extract_documents_from_media` — a soft "watch this" rather than an urgent split. `runtime/hooks/context.py`'s `TurnContext` (~28 fields) is the related carrier-dataclass pressure point (CTX-001).

**The "junk-drawer top level" question.** The privacy package root holds several single-concern top-level modules (`provider_egress_gate.py`, `compaction.py`, `compaction_provider.py`, `egress_policy.py`, `tool_models.py`, `visual_redaction.py`, `visual_egress_gate.py`, `document_redaction.py`). These are individually cohesive, but the flat root mixes egress gating, compaction, visual redaction, and shared models at the same level as `core/` and `runtime/`. A light regrouping (`egress/`, `visual/`, with `tool_models.py` and the proposed `visual_models.py` as dependency-free boundary types) would make the dependency direction inspectable and remove three lazy-import workarounds (the `VisualVaultEntry` cycle described in the visual-redaction redundancy map).

**Over-fragmentation is the mirror image of the god-modules.** `runtime/registry.py` (16 lines) + `runtime/routing.py` (18 lines) split one dispatch job across two single-function modules, restating the supported-intent set three times (`Intent` enum, `_WORKERS` keys, `_SUPPORTED_INTENTS`) — merge into one routing module and derive the set from `_WORKERS.keys()` (AR-001, AR-002). Several one-line indirection wrappers (`_build_system_prompt`, `extract_math_expression`→`_extract_expression`, `_ocr_text_words`) add surface without value (RD-001, math-010, VR-004).

**Public-API hygiene is the most systematic structural smell.** Multiple modules export dead or misaligned surfaces:
- `protocol/contracts.py` defines a large aspirational schema (`TurnContract`, `AgentTaskContract`/`AgentTaskSpec`, `ToolInvocationContract`/`ToolSpec`/`ToolPrivacySpec`, `TurnContextPayload`) with **zero production references**, which `protocol/__init__.py` actively re-exports in `__all__` — while the genuinely-used `build_turn_timeline`/`get_metrics_snapshot`/`SessionTraceIndex` are *not* exported and reached via deep submodule paths (PROTO-001, PROTO-002). The public surface advertises a protocol no code executes.
- `core/detection/llm_json.py` exports five helpers (`parse_bool`, `parse_should_sanitize`, `parse_numeric`, `normalize_var_name`, `find_numeric_substring`) with zero callers (DC-001); `core/math/math_helpers.py` exports the dead `replace_symbolic_variables` and the test-only `extract_python_snippet`/`remove_python_snippets` (math-004, math-005).
- `visual_redaction.py` (no `__all__` at all) and `webui/__init__.py` (missing `WebUIUserAttachment`/`WebUIUserDocument`/`WebUIAttachment`) sit at the opposite end (VR-002, webui-api-003).

There is **no `BaseAgent` over-abstraction inside this kernel** — the verification located it upstream (the intent analyzer's `JsonCompletionRunner` and the `BaseAgent` ABC worker shim that the runtime *consumes* rather than owns). The in-kernel analog is `AliasResolverAgent` (`core/sanitization/alias_resolver.py:12-97`): a stateless single-method class wrapped in a `_RESOLVER` singleton and reached only through a free function, whose `…Agent` name implies an LLM abstraction it is not (pure lexical matching) — collapse to a module-level function (SAN-006).

### Proposed directory shape

```
cloakbot/privacy/
├── core/
│   ├── placeholders.py        # NEW: single placeholder grammar (PLACEHOLDER_RE, TOKEN_RE, PLACEHOLDER_TAG_RE)
│   ├── types.py
│   ├── detection/             # facade + detectors + chunking (clean today)
│   ├── math/                  # arithmetic kernel (clean today)
│   ├── sanitization/
│   └── state/
│       ├── vault.py           # registry only
│       ├── scope.py           # SPLIT from vault: ephemeral-scope routing
│       └── vault_store.py     # SPLIT from vault: disk + artifact I/O
├── egress/                    # GROUP: provider_egress_gate, egress_policy, compaction, compaction_provider
├── visual/                    # SPLIT visual_redaction.py into a package
│   ├── detector.py
│   ├── ocr_match.py
│   ├── render.py
│   ├── pipeline.py
│   ├── visual_models.py       # NEW: dependency-free VisualVaultEntry/VisualBlocksResult (kills lazy imports)
│   └── egress_gate.py         # from visual_egress_gate.py
├── runtime/                   # merge registry.py + routing.py; extract attachments.py from pipeline.py
├── protocol/                  # purge dead contracts; fix __all__; unify event recorder state
├── prompting/  hooks/  transparency/  webui/
└── tool_models.py
```

## Readability & Conciseness

**Readability is the kernel's strongest dimension** (mostly 4/5, WebUI 5/5). Module docstrings are precise and threat-model-first; `runtime/streaming_restorer.py:1-105` and `streaming_sanitizer.py` are the reference shape — small, one responsibility, an invariant-stating docstring (the byte-for-byte-equal-to-`restore_tokens(full)` proof), and explicit `__all__`. Use them as the template when splitting `pipeline.py`/`visual_redaction.py` (RT-010).

Recurring readability drags:
- **Essay-length comment blocks dwarf the code** they annotate: `vault.py:354-405` (~50 lines of Cap-B narrative over a one-line `Literal`), `handler.py:80-118` (~25 lines of Plan-C narrative inside one `for` body), `sanitize.py:47-53`. Move the durable design narrative into `docs/domains/privacy.md` and leave a one-line pointer (SAN-008).
- **`emit_event` boilerplate obscures control flow** in `pipeline.py:165-272,617-678`: ~10 calls each repeat `trace_id=/span_id=/session_id=/turn_id=/stage=/status=`, and the STARTED/FAILED/SUCCEEDED triple is copy-pasted three times around sanitize/dispatch/restore. A ctx-bound emitter + a `with span(name, stage, payload):` context manager collapses each triple to one block (RT-001).
- **`Any` hides knowable contracts**: `provider_egress_gate.py` types `messages`/`fallback_preset`/`__init__` params as `Any` though a concrete `ModelPresetConfig` exists (ECM-03); `visual_redaction.py` declares `redacted_blocks: list[Any]` while only ever producing dicts (VR-011); `tool_models.py` carries the privacy-critical sanitized-vs-restored args as bare `dict[str, Any]` (ECM-06). `PlaceholderResolver = "Callable[...]"` is a string literal masquerading as a type alias and is never referenced (VR-005).
- **Long functions doing too much**: `apply_privacy_math_for_turn` (`math_executor.py:126-195`) mixes parsing/execution/vault-mutation/dual-stream assembly (math-008); `_matching_text_word_boxes` carries three matching strategies with magic 70%/8-char/≥3 thresholds as bare literals (VR-008); `compaction.py:216-295` `finalize()` re-derives `smap` and re-implements the foreign-token check (ECM-10).

### Cross-file redundancy map

| # | Redundancy | Files | Consolidation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Placeholder/token grammar** (7+ copies, 81 grep hits) | `vault.py`, `handler.py`, `restorer.py`, `provider_egress_gate.py`, `detector.py`, `digit_detector.py`, `general_detector.py`, `math_executor.py` | One `core/placeholders.py` (search + anchored-capture + tag-capture variants) — *SAN-002/ECM-05/DUP-001* |
| 2 | **Span-overlap replacement** logic | `handler.py:14-39`, `vault.py:315-345` | `replace_non_overlapping(text, needle, replacement)` — *SAN-004* |
| 3 | **Placeholder→entity_type extraction** | `restorer.py:164-168` re-implements `vault._placeholder_tag`∘`_entity_type_from_tag` | one `entity_type_from_placeholder(token)` in vault — *SAN-003* |
| 4 | **Entity parsers** (`parse_digit_entities`/`parse_general_entities`) share load→seen→validate→token-filter→`find` loop + identical `except` handler | `digit_detector.py:76-103`, `general_detector.py:256-291` | shared `iter_valid_entities(raw, prompt, valid_types, build_fn)` — *DUP-002* |
| 5 | **Snippet regex** byte-identical | `math_executor.py:20-23`, `math_helpers.py:10-13` | own once in `math_helpers.py` — *math-001* |
| 6 | **Whitespace/CRLF cleanup** (`_clean_output`/`_light_touch_cleanup`) | `math_executor.py:316`, `math_helpers.py:168-171` | one `normalize_whitespace` — *math-002* |
| 7 | **Sorted-by-length `\b`-regex substitution** loop (×3) | `math_helpers.py:152-157,160-165`, `math_executor.py:300-302` | `_substitute_token_names(text, mapping)` — *math-003* |
| 8 | **Token-prefix semantics** encoded twice (prompt block vs `_describe_numeric_token`) | `math_executor.py:92-100`, `:268-287` | one ordered prefix→description mapping — *math-007* |
| 9 | **`ToolPrivacyRecord`** field list (×2) | `tool_interceptor.py:342-353,440-451` | `_append_tool_record` — *RT-002* |
| 10 | **`ToolApprovalRequest`** build-append-raise (×2) | `tool_interceptor.py:246-260,274-288` | `_raise_for_approval` — *RT-003* |
| 11 | **Message-content flattening** (`_stringify_messages` vs `_window_text_from_messages`) | `provider_egress_gate.py:59-87`, `compaction_provider.py:45-56` | `flatten_message_text(messages, *, roles=None)` — *ECM-04* |
| 12 | **image_url block detection / data-url extraction** (3 ternaries + 5 isinstance guards) | `visual_redaction.py:227,254,291,…`, `visual_egress_gate.py:180-181` | `_is_image_block`/`_image_url_of` helpers — *VR-007* |
| 13 | **mime→suffix** mapping (4-branch fn vs hardcoded `.png`) | `visual_redaction.py:943-950`, `visual_egress_gate.py:189` | one dict beside `detect_image_mime` — *VR-009* |
| 14 | **`int((a-b).total_seconds()*1000)` clamped** (×3) | `metrics.py:28`, `observability.py:64`, `replay.py:66` | `_elapsed_ms(start, end)` helper — *protocol redundancy* |
| 15 | **Manual tally-by-key** vs `Counter` | `metrics.py:20-34` vs `report.py` | standardize on `collections.Counter` — *METRICS-001* |
| 16 | **Dead value-parsers** (`find_numeric_substring`/`parse_numeric` re-implement currency stripping; both unused) | `llm_json.py` | delete — *DC-001* |

## PEP8 & Idiom

**Today's lint is clean but minimal.** `ruff check cloakbot/privacy/ --statistics` exits 0 — the *current* config catches nothing. The latent debt surfaces only under a stricter ruleset, and a handful of genuine idiom issues hide beneath the green check:

- **Non-idiomatic membership test:** `prompt.find(text) == -1` used purely as a containment check at `digit_detector.py:94` and `general_detector.py:276` — should be `text not in prompt` (RD-002).
- **Legacy typing imports:** `core/types.py:4` imports `Dict`/`List`/`Union` and uses `List[str]`/`Dict[str, str]`/`Union[…]` despite `from __future__ import annotations` — the lone hold-out; switch to `list[...]`/`dict[...]`/`X | Y` (`DetectedEntity = GeneralEntity | ComputableEntity`) (SAN-009).
- **Broad `except Exception`** (25 occurrences, 12 flagged BLE001): `vault._load_map:684-685` swallows refactor bugs as "corrupt session map" — narrow to `(json.JSONDecodeError, ValidationError, OSError)` (SAN-010); the `pipeline.py` config gates (`110-117,132-139`) and `sanitize.py` fail-open path are *intentional* fail-safe defaults but should leave a debug/warning breadcrumb (RT-006, SAN-010).
- **`getattr` over a non-discriminated Union:** `handler.py:88` `getattr(entity, "dedupe_hint", None)` defends against the type system; the comment at `types.py:225` ("Discriminated Union for Pydantic v2") is inaccurate since `DetectedEntity` is a plain `Union` with no `Field(discriminator=...)` — make it `isinstance(entity, GeneralEntity)` or add a real `Literal` discriminator (SAN-005).
- **`Path` not used idiomatically:** `webui/history.py:24-25,37,64` uses builtin `open()` + string-concat newlines despite `_turns_path` returning a `Path` (webui-idiom-005).
- **Hand-rolled counting** where `Counter` fits (METRICS-001); **`import dataclasses`/`import uuid` inside function bodies** (`compaction_provider.py`, `visual_egress_gate.py:287`) — move stdlib imports to module scope (ECM-07, VR-010).

### Recommended ruff configuration

Adopt an opt-out posture (`select = ["ALL"]`, then `ignore` what you reject) rather than the current opt-in minimum. Under `--select ALL` the kernel shows **697 errors (117 auto-fixable)**. Phase it:

**Phase 1 — auto-fixable + zero-judgment (turn on now):**
- `COM812` (63, missing-trailing-comma), `TC001` (35, typing-only first-party import), `UP` (PEP-604/585 upgrades) — these largely `ruff --fix` themselves and force the `core/types.py` modernization.
- `E501` (86) with `line-length = 100`, but `# noqa: E501` the embedded-prompt lines in the detector files (`general_detector.py`, `digit_detector.py`) rather than reflowing prompt text.

**Phase 2 — safety + quality (turn on, fix manually):**
- `BLE001` (12, blind-except) — forces the `except Exception` narrowing above.
- `PLR2004` (19, magic-value) — forces the VR/math threshold constants (`_FUZZY_RATIO`, `1e-12`, `.10g`).
- `PLC0415` (12, import-outside-top-level) — forces the stdlib-import hoists.
- `ANN401` (47, `Any`-typed) — forces the `provider_egress_gate`/`tool_models`/`visual_redaction` typing honesty.
- `C901 max-complexity = 10`, `PLR0911/0912/0913` — flags the 6 complex functions (`_redact_image` 16, `_stringify_messages` 13) as the refactor targets.

**Phase 3 — docstrings (the largest class, ~245 D-series):**
- Enable `D` with `convention = "google"` but scope it (`per-file-ignores` for tests). 210 undocumented public symbols is real, but most are low-value; gate it on the *new* package structure so you document boundaries, not internals.
- `INP001` (19, implicit namespace package) — add the missing `__init__.py` files as part of the directory regrouping.

## Objective Metrics

Scope: 59 `.py` files, 10,341 LOC, 368 functions. All via `uv run ruff` (no installs).

| Signal | Value | Note |
|---|---|---|
| `ruff check` (current config) | **exit 0** | clean today |
| `ruff --select ALL` | **697 errors** (117 auto-fixable) | latent debt |
| TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX | **0** | genuinely none (word-boundary grep) |
| Functions with `->` return annotation | 282 / 368 ≈ **77%** | |
| `Any`-typed annotations (ANN401) | **47** | `tool_interceptor.py` 10, `provider_egress_gate.py` 10, `visual_egress_gate.py` 6 |
| Undocumented public symbols (D100–D107) | ≈ **210** | class 62 / method 53 / func 49 / module 27 / init 19 |
| Broad `except Exception` | **25** | `pipeline.py` 7, `visual_redaction.py` 3, `vault.py` 3 |
| Placeholder-regex `<<…>>` occurrences | **81** | the duplication-1 hotspot |

**Strict ruleset top categories:** E501 86, COM812 63, D101 62, D102 53, D103 49, ANN401 47, TC001 35, D100 27, INP001 19, PLR2004 19, BLE001 12, PLC0415 12.

**Cyclomatic complexity (C901 > 10):**

| Function | File:Line | Complexity |
|---|---|---:|
| `_redact_image` | `visual_redaction.py:398` | 16 |
| `_stringify_messages` | `core/sanitization/handler.py:42` | 13 |
| `process_visual_blocks` | `visual_redaction.py:771` | 12 |
| `_matching_text_word_boxes` | `visual_redaction.py:697` | 11 |
| `sniff_content_type` | `core/detection/chunking/sniffer.py:34` | 11 |
| `apply_tokens` | `provider_egress_gate.py:59` | 11 |

**Largest files:** `visual_redaction.py` 1156 (≈2.5× any other; 3/6 complexity hits, 11 E501, 5 ANN401), `core/state/vault.py` 838, `runtime/tool_interceptor.py` 723, `runtime/pipeline.py` 686.

## PydanticAI Migration Roadmap

**No `pydantic_ai` dependency exists today** — `pyproject.toml` lists only `pydantic`/`pydantic-settings`. Every "reinvents PydanticAI" finding is therefore a *migration opportunity*, not a present defect, and adoption adds a dependency plus reworks the local-vLLM call path. The kernel is unusually well-positioned for the rewrite because the trust boundary is already expressed as structured-JSON-only crossings, which map directly onto typed `Agent(output_type=...)` results.

**Where it maps cleanly (highest value):**
- **The hand-rolled LLM-JSON runners are the prime targets.** `core/detection/llm_json.py`'s `JsonCompletionRunner` manually builds chat messages, sets `response_format={"type":"json_object"}`, hand-rolls a single retry on `is_valid_json_object` failure, and `load_json_object`/`extract_json`/`strip_think_block` manually strip ```` ```json ```` fences and `<think>` blocks before `json.loads` — then both detectors validate the dict into pydantic models by hand (PA-001). This is exactly `Agent(output_type=DigitEntities | GeneralEntities)`: PydanticAI owns message construction, JSON-mode, validation, and retry, **deleting** `JsonCompletionRunner`, `extract_json`/`load_json_object`/`is_valid_json_object`, and most of `parse_digit_entities`/`parse_general_entities`. The same shape recurs in `visual_redaction._inspect_visual` + `_parse_model_json` (VR-006), which maps onto `Agent(output_type=DetectorResult)` with a `SensitiveItem` model, deleting the fence/think stripping and `json_repair` fallback.
- **Post-validation privacy filters become output-validators.** The `prompt.find` containment check, `_TOKEN_PATTERN` filter, and dedupe logic survive as a thin PydanticAI output-validator — preserving the trust boundary (still local model, structured output only) while removing the hand-rolled parsing layer.
- **The egress/fallback glue maps onto `FallbackModel`.** The private-attribute reach-through into `FallbackProvider` (`provider._primary`/`_fallback_presets`/`_provider_factory` at `provider_egress_gate.py:183-188`) and the save/restore-around-`super()` preset scoping (`:147-166`) are exactly what a PydanticAI `FallbackModel` exposes publicly as `.models` (ECM-01, ECM-02). The `__getattr__`-delegating `CompactionGuardedProvider` (`compaction_provider.py:81-95`) is the kind of transparent shim a typed `Model` wrapper/middleware replaces (ECM-09).

**Where it does NOT apply (keep as local code):**
- **Math execution is not LLM-driven.** `apply_privacy_math_for_turn` executes `result = <expr>` against Vault values under an AST allowlist — PydanticAI applies only upstream (the `math_agent` worker that *emits* snippets), not to the local arithmetic kernel. Do not route arithmetic through an Agent.
- **The `BaseAgent` ABC worker shim and intent analyzer** live upstream of this kernel; migrate them with the agent layer, not as part of the privacy rewrite.

**Sequencing principle:** centralize the placeholder grammar and delete dead code *first* (so the migration ports one regex and validates fewer dicts), then convert detectors to Agents, then the visual detector, then the provider/fallback layer last (highest blast radius, security-relevant).

## Recommended Action Plan

Quick wins (S) → structural (M) → PydanticAI (L), sequenced so each phase de-risks the next.

**Phase A — Quick wins / pure deletion (do first; near-zero risk):**
1. **(S)** Delete dead exports: five `llm_json` helpers (DC-001), `replace_symbolic_variables` + test-only snippet exports (math-004/005), the dead `token_order` pass and `_ocr_text_words` in `visual_redaction.py` (VR-003/004), and `PlaceholderResolver` string-alias (VR-005). *Removes ~120 lines of maintained-but-unused surface.*
2. **(S)** Purge the dead `protocol/contracts.py` schema (`TurnContract`/`AgentTaskContract`/…) and fix `protocol/__init__.py` `__all__` to export what production imports (PROTO-001/002).
3. **(S)** Idiom auto-fixes: `text not in prompt` (RD-002), modernize `core/types.py` typing (SAN-009), hoist in-body stdlib imports (ECM-07, VR-010), `Counter` in `metrics.py` (METRICS-001), `Path.open` in `webui/history.py` (webui-idiom-005). Land alongside the Phase-1 ruff rules.
4. **(S)** Add `__all__` to `visual_redaction.py` and the three missing WebUI exports (VR-002, webui-api-003); add the WebUI redactor field-coverage test (webui-sec-001).

**Phase B — Centralization (the highest-leverage structural work):**
5. **(M)** Create `core/placeholders.py` and route all 7+ regex copies + 81 grep hits through it; delete `_IS_PLACEHOLDER_RE`, the egress-gate copy, and the restorer literal (SAN-002, ECM-05, DUP-001). **Do this before the migration** — it shrinks what the rewrite must port.
6. **(M)** Extract the shared span-overlap replacer (SAN-004) and `entity_type_from_placeholder` (SAN-003); these are the privacy-critical dedup wins.
7. **(M)** De-duplicate the tool-interceptor builders: `_append_tool_record` (RT-002), `_raise_for_approval` (RT-003), and the `SanitizeResult` dataclass replacing the 5-tuple fold (RT-005).
8. **(M)** Collapse the math duplication: one snippet regex, one `normalize_whitespace`, one `_substitute_token_names`, one prefix→description mapping (math-001/002/003/007).
9. **(M)** Introduce the ctx-bound emitter + `span()` context manager in `pipeline.py` (RT-001); merge `registry.py`+`routing.py` (AR-001/002); collapse `AliasResolverAgent` to a function (SAN-006).

**Phase C — Module splits (legibility for the rewrite):**
10. **(L)** Split `visual_redaction.py` into a `visual/` package + dependency-free `visual_models.py` (VR-001, removes 3 lazy imports). Use the streaming modules as the template.
11. **(M)** Split `vault.py` into `vault.py`/`scope.py`/`vault_store.py` behind the `get_map`/`save_map` facade (SAN-001); narrow `_load_map`'s except (SAN-010).
12. **(M)** Extract `runtime/attachments.py` from `pipeline.py` (RT-004); move the essay comment blocks into `docs/domains/privacy.md` (SAN-008).
13. **(S)** Flip ruff to opt-out (`select = ["ALL"]`) Phases 1–2; defer the 245 docstring items (Phase 3) to land against the *new* structure.

**Phase D — PydanticAI migration (after A–C; highest blast radius):**
14. **(L)** Add `pydantic-ai`; convert the two detectors to `Agent(output_type=…)` with typed entity models, keeping the privacy filters as output-validators (PA-001). Deletes most of `llm_json.py` and both parsers.
15. **(L)** Convert `visual_redaction._inspect_visual` to `Agent(output_type=DetectorResult)` (VR-006).
16. **(L)** Replace the `FallbackProvider` reach-through and preset save/restore with `FallbackModel.models`; replace the `__getattr__` provider shim with a typed `Model` wrapper (ECM-01/02/09). **Last** — security-relevant fail-closed behavior must be re-verified against the egress evals after this change.

Reports referenced are by ID throughout; all file:line citations were spot-confirmed against the working tree (`visual_redaction.py` 1156 LOC, `vault.py` 838, the placeholder-regex copies at `vault.py:38-39`, `handler.py:11`, `provider_egress_gate.py:34`, `detector.py:25`, `digit_detector.py:13`).
